objective: Make --shout upper-case both formal and informal greetings by changing the hello.py:15 guard from `if shout and not formal:` to `if shout:`, and add a standalone CliRunner regression script.
key_changes: hello.py:15 guard changed from `if shout and not formal:` to `if shout:` so shouting applies on the formal path too.
Net git diff of hello.py is empty: HEAD already has `if shout:`; the working tree reintroduced the bug, so the fix restores HEAD.
Add standalone regression guard tests/test_shout_formal.py (no pytest available; click 8.1.6 is) run via `python3 tests/test_shout_formal.py`, exiting non-zero on failure.

Regression asserts: `--formal --shout` -> `GREETINGS, ALICE
`; `--shout` -> `HELLO, ALICE!
`; `--formal` -> `Greetings, Alice
`; `--formal --shout --count 2` repeats the shouted formal line.
Classification: business-rule defect — conditional encoded the wrong rule for when shouting applies.
The regression test is the substantive new artifact of this fix, not the hello.py edit.
